stimulus:
[Line graph: Total Conference Paper Submissions by Topic, 2017–2020. Vertical: Number of submissions, 0–400. Horizontal: 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020.]

  • Computer science: 2017 ~180, 2018 ~210, 2019 ~280, 2020 ~340
  • Education: 2017 ~150, 2018 ~140, 2019 ~155, 2020 ~150
  • Psychology: 2017 ~120, 2018 ~135, 2019 ~145, 2020 ~160

A conference coordinator wrote, “Submissions to one topic grew dramatically during this period, while another remained nearly flat.”
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the graph to support the underlined claim?

answerOptions.content: Computer science submissions nearly doubled from 2017 (about 180) to 2020 (about 340), while education submissions stayed near 150 throughout the period.
In 2018, psychology submissions were higher than education submissions.
In 2020, the total number of computer science submissions was greater than that of psychology submissions.
The lowest number of psychology submissions in a year was about 120 in 2017.

rationale:
Choice A is correct because it shows one topic (computer science) growing dramatically (~180 to ~340) and another (education) staying nearly flat (~150 throughout), directly supporting both parts of the claim.

Choice B is incorrect: it compares two topics in a single year, not growth over time. Choice C is incorrect: it again compares topics in a single year. Choice D is incorrect: it gives only the minimum of one topic, not contrast between dramatic growth and flatness.
